preserveUserBrands: assets/brands/* except assets/brands/example/ (example is updatable, user brands are not)

featureFlags._doc: Runtime feature flags read at Electron startup. Do NOT rely on these for security-critical defaults — a stale version.json on an offline client stays at the shipped values. Security-critical changes must land as code pushes with a version bump.

notes:
v1.18.9: Postscript + AppLovin tile save fix — paying-user incident. Clicking Save in the Magic-panel modal for Postscript (or AppLovin's MAX/AppDiscovery report keys) silently did nothing — no tile turn green, no error toast, modal just stayed open. Root cause was a v1.18.0 ship omission: when the Postscript and AppLovin BYOK tiles were added to renderer.js's API_KEY_PLATFORMS map, the corresponding entries in `oauth-persist.js`'s CONFIG_FIELD_ALLOWLIST and VAULT_SENSITIVE_KEYS were never added. main.js's save-config-field IPC handler returned `{success:false, error:'Unknown config field'}`, and the renderer's failure path tried to open a NEW error modal that queued behind the still-active input modal and never displayed — looking exactly like 'nothing happened'.

Three-layer fix:

(1) Added `postscriptApiKey`, `applovinMaxReportKey`, `applovinAdReportKey` to BOTH CONFIG_FIELD_ALLOWLIST and VAULT_SENSITIVE_KEYS in oauth-persist.js. Each entry carries a REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-27) comment with the incident writeup.

(2) Strengthened the oauth-persist regression test with a NEW guard: parses renderer.js's API_KEY_PLATFORMS object literal (depth-1 brace match) and asserts every BYOK tile key is in BOTH lists. The previous guard only caught drift in one direction (allowlist key missing from vault list); this catches the other direction (renderer tile missing from allowlist) which is exactly how Postscript shipped broken in v1.18.0. A future BYOK tile addition that forgets either list now fails CI before merge.

(3) Fixed the silent-failure UX: replaced the modal-on-modal error display in the API-key save flow with `showModalError`, the inline pattern every other tile save (Shopify, Meta manual key, etc.) already uses. A save rejection now displays an inline error in the still-open input modal so the user can correct or report.

Also fixed: pre-existing manifest drift on `app/auth-credentials.js` (added by a recent silent-refresh change but never registered in version.json's updatable array). Bootstrapper-installed users would have missed every future change to that file. Picked up incidentally because the updatable-coverage CI guard caught it during this release.

2 new tests + 1 strengthened test (every API_KEY_PLATFORMS key reaches both lists, Postscript + AppLovin keys present in both). 715/715 app tests green.

v1.18.8: Cross-brand connection-leak fix — paying-user incident. A newly-added brand could see another brand's Meta / TikTok / Google / Shopify / Stripe / Klaviyo / Reddit / LinkedIn / Etsy / Amazon / Pinterest tiles render as 'connected' in the Magic panel, and worse, the Go binary could pick up that other brand's tokens at action time and route ad pushes / dashboard pulls through the wrong account. Five-layer fix, all carry REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-27) tags + source-scan or behavioral tests:

(1) readBrandConfig (main.js) used to mutate the cached global config object via Object.assign onto readConfig()'s shared reference. Each call polluted the cache with whichever brand was queried last. Fixed by spread-cloning before mutating: const cfg = { ...readConfig() }.

(2) Vault placeholder resolution used to fall back unconditionally to the _global namespace. New UNIVERSAL_KEYS set (falApiKey, elevenLabsApiKey, heygenApiKey, arcadsApiKey, foreplayApiKey, googleApiKey, slack*, discord*) gates the fallback: brand-scoped keys (Meta/TikTok/Google/Shopify/Stripe/Klaviyo/Reddit/LinkedIn/Etsy/Amazon/Pinterest tokens) NEVER fall back to _global. Unresolved brand-scoped placeholders are stripped so the binary can't accidentally send '@@VAULT:metaAccessToken@@' as an Authorization header.

(3) getConnections used to call the merging readBrandConfig. Switched to buildStrictBrandConfig (which strips every BRAND_KEYS field from the global base before overlaying brand creds) and rewrote checkBrand to read brandCfg ONLY when a brand is named — never coalesce brandCfg[key] || globalCfg[key]. Added LinkedIn + Pinterest checks.

(4) Go-side walkAndResolve (vault.go) had the same _global fallback. New brandScopedKeys map mirrors the JS list — vault resolution refuses to fall back for any of those keys, and unresolved brand-scoped placeholders collapse to empty string. New IsBrandScopedVaultKey exported predicate.

(5) Renderer loadConnections had no sequence guard — rapid brand switches could let a slow brand-A response paint over brand-C's tiles. Added _connLoadSeq token + brand-selector double-check on resolve.

Tests: 16 new (10 source-scan in JS, 8 behavioral in Go: POG vs IvoryElla cross-resolution, brand-scoped no-fallback, universal-fallback-still-works, unresolved-collapses-to-empty). 711/711 app tests + full go test ./... green (166s).

v1.18.7: Creative Eye OS — batch review at scale. Two surfaces redesigned end-to-end so a paying user can fly through hundreds of generated creatives in one sitting: (1) chat-rendered artifact galleries no longer dominate the bubble with an open grid — every batch collapses to a fanned stack of 1–4 cards (transformGalleryToStack in app/gallery-viewer.js) with a click-to-expand affordance into a new full-screen viewer; (2) the Archive tab gains Lightroom-grade culling — full multi-select (shift-range, ctrl/cmd-toggle, Ctrl-A, Esc to clear), bulk trash that goes to the OS Recycle Bin / macOS Trash via shell.trashItem (no more hard rm -rf catastrophes — recovery is one click in the OS shell), keep/reject ★/✗ flags persisted to a results/.flags.json sidecar, and a live fs.watch on results/ that reflects external file changes without a manual refresh. The shared full-screen viewer (gv-* in style.css) is the centerpiece: keyboard-first navigation (←/→/↑/↓ + Home/End), Space toggles ★ keep, R toggles ✗ reject (with auto-advance for hammer-rejection power-users), Delete/Backspace/X moves to Trash, Esc closes. Touch + trackpad swipe via PointerEvents (threshold + velocity-aware). Filmstrip is virtualized via IntersectionObserver-style windowing — 5,000 items render in O(1) visible thumbs, no DOM bloat. Selection capacity went from 2 (legacy merge-only) to unlimited; the existing 'Generate in my style' merge button still surfaces when exactly 2 items are selected and at least one is a swipe-card.

New modules: app/results-flags.js (sidecar I/O + atomic .tmp+rename, 15 tests), app/archive-multiselect.js (pure selection state machine — anchor-based shift-range, ctrl-toggle, drag-rect helpers; 21 tests), app/results-watcher.js (recursive fs.watch on Win/Mac with polling fallback, debounced; 9 tests), app/gallery-viewer.js (full-screen viewer + transformGalleryToStack; 16 source-scan + DOM-stub tests). main.js: shell.trashItem replaces fs.rm with a layered REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-26) note explaining why hard delete + bulk select was a paying-user catastrophe; new IPC handlers archive-flags-get / archive-flags-set / archive-flags-set-bulk; results-watcher mounted on window create + torn down in before-quit alongside briefingWatcher. preload.js: getArchiveFlags / setArchiveFlag / setArchiveFlagsBulk / onArchiveChanged bridges. renderer.js: gallery transformation runs after every renderMarkdown finalize (both fact-binding-on and -off branches); legacy chat lightbox guard added so .merlin-gallery-stack and .gv-viewer images route to the viewer instead; archive multi-select model wired with onChange-driven UI sync; selection toolbar (★ Keep / ✗ Reject / 🗑 Trash / Clear) appears above the grid when selection > 0; archive cards open the viewer over the entire visible filtered set with the clicked card as start index. index.html: archive-multiselect.js + gallery-viewer.js script tags. style.css: ~250 lines for stack (perspective fan with 4 staggered cards, hover lifts the top), viewer (grid layout for toolbar/stage/strip), filmstrip thumbs with current/keep/reject states, selection toolbar, light-theme parity. 61 new tests, 0 regressions.

v1.18.6: Google Ads connection — bulletproof. Single customer-visible fix targeting the second occurrence of the live incident on Ivory Ella brand: clicking "check the adwords campaigns now" on v1.18.5 hit "googleAdsCustomerId required — run google-login first", and the agent then asked the user to paste their 10-digit customer ID by hand. Violated the "no manual tokens, zero API key hunting" promise.

Root cause: PR #77 wired the customer-ID auto-discovery only into runGoogleAdsStatus. Every other google-ads handler (Setup, Push, Insights, Kill, Duplicate, CAPI offline conversion upload, watchdog health probe) still bare-fataled on empty customerId without trying to recover. The agent forwarded the raw "required — run google-login first" string to the user; the renderer's friendlyError had no chip pattern to upgrade it, so the agent improvised a "paste your customer ID" prompt.

Fix: ensureGoogleAdsCustomerID(cfg) is now the SINGLE chokepoint every Google Ads code path goes through. It (a) short-circuits when the customer ID is already saved, (b) auto-reconciles when only the access token is present (refreshes the BFF-injected dev token from the vault, calls listAccessibleGoogleCustomers, persists the first ID), (c) returns precise actionable Reconnect-driven errors on every failure mode, with a [[chip:Reconnect Google Ads:reconnect:google]] sentinel that renderer.js friendlyError upgrades into a one-click reconnect button. Wired into googleAdsRequest (covers every API call), every run* handler in googleads.go, googleOfflineConversionUpload in capi.go, and checkGoogleAdsHealth in watchdog.go. A source-scan regression test (TestNoBareCustomerIDFatalInGoogleAdsHandlers) fails CI if a future handler skips the chokepoint.

User flow after this lands: worst case is a 1-click recovery — click "check campaigns" → ensureGoogleAdsCustomerID auto-discovers and persists customer ID → campaigns shown. If discovery fails (token expired, dev token missing, wrong Google account, etc.), the chip-bearing error string becomes a "Reconnect Google Ads" button. One click → fresh OAuth → next "check campaigns" works. No more "paste your 10-digit customer ID" prompts. Period.

15 new tests cover: chokepoint enforcement (source-scan), every error branch carries the chip sentinel, byte-exact chip-format lock, dev-token-missing / dev-not-approved / token-expired / 429-rate-limited / no-accessible-accounts, hermetic-vault setup, and the access-token-but-no-customer-id case now triggers discovery instead of a bare error. Full go test ./... green (166s, 0 failures).

v1.18.5: Ad-preview reliability + landing-page rate-limit hardening. One customer-visible Go-side change: clicking an ad in the dashboard now reliably shows the creative image, even after Meta's CDN signed URLs expire. Previously every ad-detail click hit a Meta scontent.fbcdn.net URL whose signed token had a ~24h TTL — between insights pulls the URL would silently expire and the preview modal rendered the "thumbnail URL has expired or the file is no longer on disk" fallback. Live incident reported by Ryan 2026-04-24. Fix: every meta-insights pull now downloads the creative bytes to assets/brands/<brand>/.creative-cache/<adID>.<ext>, so the renderer prefers the local file over the remote URL and clicks work forever regardless of CDN TTL. Cache short-circuits on second-and-later runs (same ad ID = same creative), uses atomic .tmp + rename to survive crash mid-write, hard-caps each file at 8MB, and restricts file extensions to known image/video types so a misbehaving CDN cannot trick the cache into writing .html / .js / .exe alongside Merlin's other assets.

Server-side (already deployed live to merlin-landing, ships in this binary tag for changelog hygiene): every BFF endpoint that calls rateLimit (OAuth exchange, license check, entitlement preflight, feedback, OAuth refresh, etc.) is now backed by Cloudflare D1 instead of KV. The previous KV-backed limiter exhausted the LICENSES namespace's 1000-puts/day free-tier cap on 2026-04-24, started throwing on every put, and turned every rate-limited endpoint into a Cloudflare 1101 "Worker threw exception" — the binary saw HTTP 500, friendlyError mapped it to "X is having issues. Wait a few minutes — this is on their end", and users saw a generic "Connection Failed" modal blaming Google / Meta / etc. for a Merlin-side outage. The hot-fix wrapped rateLimit in try/catch (fails open) so the worker stays alive; the D1 migration eliminates the underlying quota issue (D1 free tier is 100k writes/day, 100x more headroom for the same brand of counter). Atomic UPSERT with RETURNING is also race-safe — concurrent calls cannot drop increments the way KV's read-modify-write could.

114/114 worker-payments tests pass + full go test ./... green (165s). Rule 16 update-manifest signing remains intentionally dormant — both pubkey constants still empty string.

v1.18.4: Security + infrastructure hardening release that consolidates everything since the v1.18.2 cut. Three customer-visible changes: (1) RFC 8252 fast-click OAuth — clicking an OAuth tile now opens the user's default browser in <50ms (shell.openExternal fires before the loopback server binds, the vault spawns, or the subprocess starts; the oauth-fast-open.js + oauth-provider-config.js helpers are now in the updatable manifest so bootstrapper-installed users get the fix via /update, not just NSIS/DMG users). (2) Google Ads connect silently dropped the customerId for a small fraction of users whose account-discovery call returned before the OAuth persist write; the fix auto-saves on discovery and reconciles stuck installs on next dashboard pull — no user action required. (3) Creative format taxonomy now covers 15 additional DTC archetypes (Demo, Transformation, Comparison, Split-Screen, UGC variants, Talking-Head, Gameplay, and others) so the tournament router and the Copy Quality Gate route briefs more precisely for vertical-specific asks. Infrastructure (invisible to users but ships in this binary): vault tmp-file suffix is now randomized (Gitar PR #112 finding — closes a predictable-path race on .vault-salt.tmp that could let a second Merlin instance blow away an in-flight salt write); landing-page blog-view counter + /api/track-click migrated from KV to Cloudflare D1 (KV free-tier 1000 puts/day was getting exhausted by a single traffic burst; D1 gives 100x write headroom); Slack fast-open now clears its "Resume" pending-record on successful connect (was leaving a stale record that made the next connect look pending-forever in the status tile); CI Zod schema stub bug that had been silently red on main was fixed. Full 598/598 tests green across app, brand-scraper, ws-server, mcp-tools, update-verifier, and bootstrapper verifier suites. Rule 16 update-manifest signing remains intentionally dormant (both pubkey constants are empty string) — bootstrap mode preserved for this release per Ryan's pre-flip checkpoint.

v1.18.3: Updatable-manifest packaging fix for the RFC 8252 fast-click OAuth files. v1.x shipped `app/oauth-fast-open.js` and `app/oauth-provider-config.js` (the two helpers that make OAuth tile clicks open the browser in <50ms — `shell.openExternal` before any subprocess spawn, vault read, or loopback server bind) but forgot to list them in `version.json`'s `updatable` array. Every bootstrapper-installed user (`MerlinSetup.exe` / `MerlinSetup`) froze on whatever version of those two files they first installed — every future OAuth change to those files was silently invisible to them via `/update` and the in-app Update toast. NSIS installer / DMG users were never affected (signed installer replaces the entire install and does not consult the manifest). Same class of bug as the v1.16.2 bootstrapper-coverage fix, same fix pattern: add the missing files to the `updatable` array and rely on the existing `app/updatable-coverage.test.js` guard to catch future regressions. That test — which source-scans `app/*.{js,html,css}` against the manifest — was already red on main when this PR was opened; a future contributor who adds a new `app/*` file and forgets the manifest will hit the same failing test in CI before merging. No behavior changes, no new tests needed (the guard test is the enforcement). Full `node --test app/*.test.js` suite 598/598 green after the manifest fix (previously 597/598 with this one red).\n\nv1.18.2: Stale-resume graceful-fail fix. The v1.18.1 release fixed the auth-error retry loop but left a second, same-shape bug open: when the Claude Agent SDK's server-side session storage no longer has the UUID we asked to resume (user cleared `~/.claude/projects`, moved machines, SDK storage format changed, session expired), the SDK throws `Claude Code returned an error result: No conversation found with session ID: <uuid>`. `main.js` already had an auto-recovery path in the for-await catch block (`isResumeFailure` → `threads.clearThread()` + silent restart) — but the regex required the literal word "session" as head noun ("session not found"), while the SDK actually emits "No conversation found" for this class of failure. That one-word mismatch defeated the recovery; the error broadcast to the renderer, `onSdkError` ran the generic 3× retry loop, every retry re-read the same stale sessionId from storage and 404'd identically, and the user landed on "Merlin tried 3 times but couldn't connect" with a useless generic "Retry Connection" button. Two-layer fix: (a) `main.js` `isResumeFailure` regex widened to match both SDK wordings surveyed in `cli.js` — "no conversation found", "conversation not found", "cannot find session/conversation" — alongside the existing "session not found" / "no such session" / "cannot resume" / "invalid session" vocabulary. The gate still requires `resumeSessionId` to be truthy so a falsy-ID request can't mis-classify an unrelated error as a recoverable resume failure; (b) `renderer.js` `onSdkError` gains an `isStaleResumeError` classifier + fail-fast branch that sits BEFORE the retry-exhausted branch and AFTER the auth fail-fast branch. On match, `_restartAttempts` zeros and a single "Start Fresh Session" button renders, wired to a NEW `merlin.startFreshSession()` preload bridge that calls a NEW `ipcMain.handle('start-fresh-session')` handler in main.js — which explicitly `threads.clearThread()` on the active brand BEFORE calling `startSession()`, so the next SDK boot has no stale UUID to re-hit. On success, the triggering user message replays automatically (same contract as the auth fail-fast). REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-24, stale-resume-graceful) comment blocks at main.js:isResumeFailure, main.js:start-fresh-session handler, and renderer.js:onSdkError cite the v1.18.1 incident text verbatim and explicitly name both SDK wordings so a future regex simplification can't silently narrow back. Test coverage: 20 new tests across `app/main-stale-resume-regex.test.js` (9 — regex extraction + behavioural fixtures for every SDK wording from `cli.js` + false-positive guards for auth/rate-limit/network errors + recovery-block structural checks + IPC-handler ordering) and `app/renderer-stale-resume-fail-fast.test.js` (11 — classifier fingerprint coverage, fail-fast ordering, button wiring to startFreshSession not startSession, replay of _lastUserMessage, preload bridge exposure, Rule 6 friendlyError pipe). Full `node --test app/*.test.js` suite 571/571 green with zero regressions. New CI step `stale-resume graceful fail lockdown` in `.github/workflows/app-unit-tests.yml` gates every PR. Same incident-driven structure as v1.18.1: non-retryable error class, fingerprint-matched classifier, fail-fast before retry, IPC handler for the cleanup-then-restart semantic, regression-guarded on both sides of the main/renderer boundary.\n\nv1.18.1: Auth-error graceful-fail fix. The v1.18.0 chat surface had two regressions when the user's Claude sign-in token expired mid-session: (1) the raw SDK auth-error payload — `{"type":"error","error":{"type":"authentication_error","message":"OAuth token has expired. Please run /login."}}` — rendered verbatim as a chat bubble, bypassing friendlyError() and violating CLAUDE.md Rule 6 ("Every user-visible error passes through friendlyError()"); (2) the renderer's onSdkError treated auth failures as generic errors and ran the 3× exponential-backoff retry loop, each attempt re-spawning the SDK session which 401'd identically, ending with a "Merlin tried 3 times but couldn't connect" card and a generic "Retry Connection" button that just re-ran the same failing boot. Paying-user incident quote 2026-04-23: "this cannot happen, we need to fail and clear gracefully." Two-layer fix: (a) `main.js` intercepts canonical SDK auth-error messages BEFORE they hit the sdk-message fan-out — new `isSdkAuthErrorMessage(msg)` + `authErrorFingerprint(text)` helpers detect the assistant-wrapper shape (`type==='assistant' && error==='authentication_failed'`), the API-error-flagged-with-auth-text shape (`isApiErrorMessage===true` + fingerprint), and the streaming text_delta fallback; the interceptor sets `_authFailureIntercepted`, freezes the pending message via `_queueFrozenForAuth`, and routes through the existing `requireAuth()` + `onAuthRequired` replay pipeline. The catch-block auth detection is widened to the same vocabulary and guarded against duplicate `requireAuth` fires. (b) `renderer.js onSdkError` gains a fail-fast branch that runs BEFORE the retry-exhausted branch: widened `isAuthError` covers "failed to authenticate" / `"type":"authentication_error"` / "please run /login" / "authentication_failed" / revoked + expired OAuth tokens; on match, the retry loop is skipped entirely, `_restartAttempts` zeros, and a single `Sign In to Claude` button is rendered that calls `merlin.triggerClaudeLogin()` (NOT `startSession()`) — on success, the user's last message replays automatically. REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-24, auth-error-graceful) comment blocks at both sites cite the incident quote and the exact fingerprint strings so a future edit can't silently narrow detection or re-introduce a retry loop on auth errors. Test coverage: 23 new tests across `app/main-auth-error-intercept.test.js` (15 — source-scan + behavioral coverage of the canonical wrapper, isApiErrorMessage+fingerprint, stream_event text_delta, rejection of billing/rate-limit/403, robustness to null/undefined/number/empty inputs) + `app/renderer-auth-error-fail-fast.test.js` (8 — isAuthError fingerprint coverage, fail-fast branch exists before retry-exhausted, calls triggerClaudeLogin not startSession, zeroes _restartAttempts, renders 'Sign In to Claude' label, renderErrorToBubble still routes through friendlyError). Full `node --test app/*.test.js` suite green with zero regressions. New CI step `auth-error graceful fail lockdown` in `.github/workflows/app-unit-tests.yml` gates every PR against these tests so the raw-401-in-a-chat-bubble + blind-retry pair can't resurface.\n\nv1.18.0: Critical launch-crash fix (supersedes v1.17.0) + AppLovin + Postscript tiles. v1.17.0 shipped with a duplicate top-level `stateFile` identifier in `app/main.js` that made the Electron main process throw `SyntaxError: Identifier 'stateFile' has already been declared` at require time — every installed user saw a native error dialog on launch and could not recover via auto-update (main process never reached the updater). This release renames the colliding identifier to `sessionStateFile`, adds dated REGRESSION GUARD blocks at both offending sites, and adds a new CI test (`app/main-no-duplicate-toplevel-ids.test.js`) that compiles main.js with `vm.Script` and source-scans for the specific identifier collision — a future edit that reintroduces the pattern fails CI before release. If you are on v1.17.0 and seeing the error dialog, reinstall from https://merlingotme.com/download/windows (Windows) or /download/mac (macOS); this v1.18.0 installer includes the fix.\n\nv1.18.0: AppLovin + Postscript tiles. Two new BYOK connectors land as first-class tiles in the Connections panel, wired end-to-end through the rate-limit preflight, vault, hook blocklist, and MCP tool surface. AppLovin exposes MAX (publisher, r.applovin.com/maxReport) and AppDiscovery (advertiser, r.applovin.com/report) reporting through a single `mcp__merlin__applovin` tool with actions `status` / `max-report` / `ad-report` / `campaign-performance` / `manage` — management intentionally returns a partner-NDA escalation error because AppLovin's campaign create/edit surface requires a signed partner agreement, not a public API key. Both report endpoints share a conservative 3/min, 60/hr, 500/day bucket (documented AppLovin limits are ~10 QPM MAX / ~4 QPM AppDiscovery; the shared-bucket default is safer than racing two independent counters against an undocumented combined cap). Reports parse both the `{"results":[]}` wrapper shape and the bare-array shape AppLovin returns for some column combinations, plus the CSV format when `format=csv`. Tile UX: the default click saves the MAX (publisher) key; right-click → "Use my API key" opens a two-step modal that accepts either/both keys (MAX + AppDiscovery) so a dual-role user doesn't need two tiles. Postscript ships with a single API-key tile (`postscriptApiKey`), a Bearer-auth request helper that routes every call through `PreflightCheck` + `RecordSuccess`/`RecordRateLimitHit` (60/min, 1500/hr, 15K/day — conservative vs the documented 15 req/sec / 900/min ceiling), cursor-paginated subscribers, campaign listing + creation, keywords + automations + shop endpoints, and — critically — every send path (both `postscript-send-campaign` and `postscript-send-message`) is REGRESSION-GUARDED to call `preflightTCPA(tz, sendAt, country, tenDLCID, consent)` BEFORE the HTTP call. Even though Postscript runs its own server-side TCPA checks, the defense-in-depth rule (CLAUDE.md Rule 4) is non-bypassable: quiet hours, revoked consent, and missing 10DLC registration all refuse before a single wire-level byte moves. Webhook verification accepts both base64 (canonical Postscript HMAC-SHA256) and hex (legacy installs) via `hmac.Equal` constant-time compare. Plumbing: `api.postscript.io` + `r.applovin.com` + `o.applovin.com` + `ms.applovin.com` added to BANNED_API_HOSTS + block-api-bypass.js so no direct outbound call can skip the binary; `applovinMaxReportKey` / `applovinAdReportKey` / `postscriptApiKey` added to the secrets-protection allowlist in main.js; AUTO_SCOPED_ACTIONS gains `applovin-max-login` / `applovin-ad-login` / `postscript-login`; `platform_login` enum gains `applovin` + `postscript` with API-key-entry instructions (no OAuth fallback — these are key-based, not OAuth); `disconnect-platform` keyMap clears both keys on AppLovin disconnect and the single Postscript key. Test coverage: 16 tests for AppLovin (URL builder, JSON wrapper + bare array + CSV parsing, 429 / 401 / 400 error paths, date-order validation, missing-key rejection, stringifyAny int-vs-float rendering) + 29 tests for Postscript (Bearer headers, endpoint-label routing, pagination + cursor + limit, E.164 validation on create, opt-out body shape, campaign list URL + sort, campaign create body, TCPA guards on both send paths covering quiet hours / revoked consent / missing 10DLC / valid send, shop array-shape vs object-shape, keywords, automations, webhook base64 + hex accept + forged reject + empty-input reject, batch executor fan-out). Full `go test ./...` green across all 39 Go files — no regressions in the existing 355+ test suite.\n\nv1.17.0: Platform + Brain upgrade. The agent itself got smarter — Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now pinned as the SDK model with a 200k autoCompactWindow and cache-event logging so long sessions stay fast and don't silently compact mid-conversation (autoCMO PR #96). The Archive tab now groups live ads by campaign so a brand running 20+ ads across 5 campaigns sees a collapsed 5-row view by default instead of a flat scroll; every group key is derived from a single source of truth to keep archive + dashboard + activity in sync (PRs #97, #98). Grid-concept-gen ships as a new creative path in the Go binary — request N variant concepts in a single tool call and the binary fans them out in parallel under the rate-limit semaphore, so a brand exploring 6 hooks × 3 formats no longer waits for a serial loop (autocmo-core PR #58). Dashboard data bundles now flow through the ``kind: dashboard`` artifact route so the renderer paints them as a proper inline report instead of a wall of JSON (autocmo-core PR #57). Security hardening: five rate-limit / CSRF bypass blockers found by an adversarial audit are closed — metaSetStatus + metaDelete now route through `metaDoWithLimit` so pause/activate/delete traffic counts against the Meta budget; Discord OAuth /callback uses `subtle.ConstantTimeCompare` for the state token; Google Ads + Amazon profile discovery do a `PreflightCheck` + `RecordSuccess`/`RecordRateLimitHit` wrap so discovery calls can't silently starve the bucket; each fix carries a REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-23) block + a source-scan test so a future refactor can't quietly revert it (autocmo-core PR #66). The vault now intercepts every sensitive key that flows through `save-config-field` IPC so no sensitive config value can be persisted in plaintext by a future IPC caller (autoCMO PR #99). IPC hardening: `main.js` gets a dated path-containment check on every filesystem handler + a paste-validation gate on the chat input so a malicious paste can't smuggle shell-metacharacters into the agent (autoCMO PR #100). Rate-limit accounting tightened in `ratelimit_preflight.go`: success paths and 429 responses now both record through the same `enforceLimitsLocked` read-only snapshot so the counter state never drifts under fan-out (autocmo-core PR #63). Landing-site rate-limit tightened to refuse spam on the `/api/update-check` endpoint before it hits the KV cache (autocmo-core PR #62). Meta Ads API: `campaignId` is now passed explicitly on every push so cross-brand multi-campaign runs can't collide; `image_url` failures surface a friendly `friendlyError()` message instead of a raw Graph response stack (autocmo-core PRs #60, #61). Google privacy disclosure page added at merlingotme.com/privacy-google-api so the Google Ads OAuth consent screen passes Google's verification checklist (autocmo-core PR #64). Release-pipeline postflight retry widened to 115s so Cloudflare's eventual-consistency window on a fresh `wrangler deploy` can't false-alarm the landing verification (autocmo-core PR #59). Main CI regression from the v1.16.5 queue fixed: `app/ws-server.test.js` now stubs the `ws` module up front so the workflow (deliberately `npm install`-less) can exercise it alongside the listen-bind source-scan guards (autoCMO PR #102). Full test suite green across both repos — every new guard carries a matching REGRESSION GUARD comment block and a corresponding test.\n\nv1.16.5: Voice-input hallucination filter — whisper.cpp's small.en-q5_1 model (and tiny.en before it) sometimes emits subtitle-credit watermarks from its training data when the audio is silence or low-SNR. Users were seeing phantom lines like 'Subs by www.zeoranger.co.uk', 'Thanks for watching!', or 'Please subscribe' appear in their chat after a near-silent recording — text they never spoke. Root cause is the Whisper language model falling back to the most common 'end of audio clip' strings it saw during training (YouTube CC files, fan-sub credits). New `app/whisper-transcript-clean.js` module sanitizes the raw stdout from whisper-cli before it returns from `transcribeAudioImpl`: strips diagnostic tags (`[BLANK_AUDIO]`, `whisper_*` logs), subtitle-credit shapes (`Subs by …`, `Subtitles: …`, `Transcribed by …`), and a curated exact-match list of known hallucinations (subscribe pitches, non-English closers, music-cue brackets). If the sanitizer leaves an empty string, the handler now returns `transcribe:empty` so the renderer coaches the retry instead of silently injecting the watermark into chat. False-positive guard: normal dictation that happens to contain 'subscribe' / 'thanks' / a real URL passes through untouched — only line-anchored subtitle-credit verbs and exact-match closers are filtered. Covers both the desktop mic button AND the PWA hold-to-record path (both route through `transcribeAudioImpl`, so one fix covers both surfaces). Test coverage: 21 new tests in `app/whisper-transcript-clean.test.js` covering the zeoranger / Amara / Subtitles-by / Transcribed-by / subscribe-pitch / non-English / music-cue / [BLANK_AUDIO] / whisper_* filters plus six false-positive guards for legitimate dictation + mixed diagnostic-and-speech inputs. Full suite green.\n\nv1.16.4: Kills the 'Transcription failed' modal endless-loop that could trap a paying user on a dead modal even after clicking OK or Cancel. Two root-cause defenses in `app/renderer.js`: (1) `showModal` now dedupes at enqueue time — if an identical (title, body/bodyHTML) modal is already visible OR already sitting in the queue, the new call is dropped instead of piling up another copy; (2) a 2 s cooldown after dismissal suppresses a re-fire of the just-closed modal, so a tight failure loop (e.g. a hardware / driver issue where every voice recording comes back `transcribe:corrupt`, or a streaming SDK error that re-fires on every retry) lands exactly one modal instead of N. Prompt-style modals (`inputPlaceholder` set) are exempt — those are interactive forms and must never coalesce. Second fix: the copy for `transcribe:empty` / `transcribe:corrupt` used to say 'hold the mic' — push-to-talk language that doesn't match Merlin's actual UX (tap once → VAD auto-stops on silence). Users who followed the instruction literally by holding the button got no effect. New copy coaches the real UX: 'Tap the mic and try again — Merlin auto-stops when you pause.' Test coverage: 8 new dedupe tests in `app/modal-dedupe.test.js` (queue-suppression, active-suppression, cooldown-window, cooldown-expiry, different-modal-after-dismiss, input-prompt exemption, 20-rapid-fire scenario) + updated assertions in `app/transcribe-error.test.js` that explicitly regression-guard against the 'hold the mic' phrase returning. REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-20, Transcription-failed-modal endless-loop incident) comment block at the top of the modal section documents both defenses so a future simplification can't revert them. Full suite green (355/355 + new tests = 363/363).

v1.16.3: Jarvis-tier voice latency pass — every piece of the voice loop got tightened so conversations feel as responsive as the best consumer voice agents. (1) VAD silence threshold dropped 700 ms → 550 ms in `app/voice-activity-detector.js`: the 2.5× adaptive RMS threshold already rejects fan hum + keyboard clicks reliably, so we can afford to cut the hang-over window without triggering false end-of-turn on mid-sentence breath pauses. 15/15 unit tests green with new dynamic frame-count derivation so the assertions stay valid across future tuning. (2) Kokoro TTS prewarm moved from a fire-and-forget `setTimeout(…, 1500)` to an immediate, awaitable `ensureTtsReady()` call at the top of `app.whenReady()` + an immediate (no setTimeout) filler-cache prewarm hooked onto that ready promise. The utility-process model load runs on its own thread and never contended with first-paint work — the 1.5 s delay was just 1.5 s of unused warmup runway. First-speak-after-launch now hits a fully hot model. (3) Cross-user prompt caching enabled on the Claude Agent SDK query by switching from `appendSystemPrompt: …` to the object-form `systemPrompt: { type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code', append: …, excludeDynamicSections: true }`. The flag strips per-user dynamic sections (working directory, auto-memory path, git status) out of the cached system-prompt prefix and re-injects them as the first user message — the static preset prefix now hits cache across every brand + every user + every fresh session, cutting first-token latency materially on cache hits. Brand/product context flows through SKILL.md + resumeSessionId, not the SDK's dynamic sections, so nothing brand-specific is affected. (4) MediaRecorder timeslice lowered 2500 ms → 1000 ms in `app/renderer.js`: a typical PTT utterance is 1.5–3 s, so at 2.5 s timeslice most sessions ended BEFORE any interim fired and the user saw nothing until whisper finished. 1 s guarantees at least one live-interim during any meaningful utterance and trims the FINAL chunk size at stop(), tightening speech-end → input.value latency. The existing `streamBusy` guard prevents overlapping whisper calls on slow machines. (5) Barge-in ramp-to-zero shortened 150 ms → 80 ms: still long enough to dodge the perceptible click of a hard audio cutoff (~40 ms floor on most voices) but short enough that the user's "stop talking to me" signal is honored in under one speech frame — indistinguishable from instant to the ear. (6) Sentence splitter gained a Jarvis-tier first-flush path: the opening clause of every response now flushes at a 30-char soft-boundary threshold (new `FIRST_FLUSH_CLAUSE_CHARS`) so audio starts on the first comma of "Okay, pulling your dashboard,…" instead of waiting ~60 more chars for the period. After the first flush, the renderer reverts to the default 80-char threshold so the body of the response stays prosodically smooth and doesn't fragment into choppy per-clause synth calls. Per-flush override keeps the splitter stateless — the renderer tracks `hasFlushedOnce` per streaming session. New unit tests (32/32 green) cover the opts override, fallback behavior on bad input, and the default-threshold baseline. Full test suite green (29 test files, covering 337+ assertions). All edits preserve existing REGRESSION GUARD behaviors (Rules 11, 13, 14 intact — no listener / BrowserWindow / timeout changes).

v1.16.2: Auto-updater coverage fix — this release repairs a silent regression that affected every user who installed Merlin via the Go bootstrapper (`MerlinSetup.exe` / `MerlinSetup`) rather than the full NSIS installer / DMG. Symptom: after clicking the in-app Update toast (or running `/update`), version.json reported "you are on v1.16.1" but the app ITSELF kept running the Electron code the user first bootstrapped with — often v1.12.0 or earlier. Every Electron-side feature that shipped between that version and v1.16.1 was invisible: v1.12.0 fact-binding (renderer intercepts + preload bridge), v1.15.0 inline artifact gallery + PWA install banner, v1.16.0 creative-angles routing in renderer.js, v1.16.1 brand-scraper timeout layers — all inert for bootstrapper users. Root cause: version.json's `updatable` array (the manifest that `downloadAndApplyUpdate` consults for the bootstrapper code path where `app.isPackaged === false`) listed `.claude/` files + brand-scraper.js + version.json, but was MISSING app/main.js, app/renderer.js, app/preload.js, every MCP module (mcp-concurrency/define-tool/envelope/errors/idempotency/jobs/meta-intent/preview/redact/server/tools), the facts subsystem (facts-cache.js + verify-facts.js + chart-renderer.js), tts-worker.js, voice-activity-detector.js, ws-server.js, the PWA LAN-fallback assets (pwa/index.html, pwa/pwa.js, pwa/sw.js), and ~20 other production Electron source files. Fix: the `updatable` array now enumerates every file that ships to a bootstrapper install. New REGRESSION GUARD test — `app/updatable-coverage.test.js` — walks the on-disk app/ + pwa/ + app/facts/ trees on every CI run and fails the build if any production file is missing from version.json\'s manifest, so a future contributor can't add a new Electron module and forget to list it for updates. Test carries an explicit EXCLUDED_FILES set for the 14 dev-only artifacts (preview harness, icon generators, pwa Cloudflare Worker, wrangler config) with a one-line justification per entry. The NSIS installer path (`app.isPackaged === true`) was never affected — it replaces the entire install with the signed installer and does not consult the manifest. Coverage: 7/7 guards green in the new test; the existing update-runner.test.js 5/5 guards remain green (terminal-visibility fix from v1.12.x intact). Note on remaining bootstrapper debt: (a) the Go bootstrapper's re-run path in `autocmo-core/bootstrapper/main.go:55` still early-returns with "already installed, launching" when the user re-runs `MerlinSetup.exe` to force-update — that's a separate fix queued for the next release and requires a Go binary rebuild; (b) this release updates the FILES listed in `updatable` but does not currently re-run `npm install` when package.json's dependency list changes between versions — a bootstrapper user whose install missed a dependency added in a past release (e.g. kokoro-js in v1.3.0) still needs to run `npm install` once in the install folder. Both items ride the normal release pipeline; neither is regressive relative to v1.16.1.\n\nv1.16.1: Onboarding hang fix — paying users on large retail sites (Forever21.com was the reproducible case) hit a permanent freeze mid-onboarding right after Merlin said "Now let me dig into the brand itself…". Root cause: `quantizeLogoColors` in `app/brand-scraper.js` injected a `fetch(logoURL, { credentials:'omit', mode:'cors' })` into the Electron BrowserWindow with NO AbortController and NO timeout. On slow / throttling / streaming image CDNs the fetch never resolved, `executeJavaScript` never returned, `scrapeBrand()` never completed, the MCP `brand_scrape` tool never responded to Claude, and the chat sat frozen with no error path. Fix is defence-in-depth: (1) the injected script now wraps the fetch in an AbortController with a 5s timeout; (2) every `executeJavaScript` call in the scraper is raced against a 15s per-call timeout via a new `executeJsWithTimeout` helper; (3) the entire `scrapeBrand()` body runs under a 90s wall-clock race that destroys the hidden BrowserWindow on timeout, unwinding any pending Electron promises; (4) the MCP `brand_scrape` handler classifies any `ScrapeTimeoutError` into a structured `TIMEOUT` envelope with a user-facing message ("scrape took too long, retry or try the apex domain") and `next_action=retry_or_split` so Claude can recover instead of spinning. New REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-20) block at the top of `brand-scraper.js` and in `CLAUDE.md` rule 13 documents the hang cause and the three timeout layers so a future "simplification" can't re-introduce the hang. Test coverage: 11 new tests in `brand-scraper.test.js` covering `raceWithTimeout`, `executeJsWithTimeout`, timeout cleanup, timer leaks, and error-shape stability; 2 new tests in `mcp-tools.test.js` covering the TIMEOUT envelope classification path and the INTERNAL_ERROR fallback for non-timeout failures. Full suite green (329/329 + 13 new = 342/342).

v1.16.0: Creative Angles — the 10-angle strategist taxonomy (Hidden Cost, Failed Solution, Social Proof Pivot, Mechanism, Enemy, Identity Shift, Urgency of Now, Comparison Flip, Objection First, Insider) is now first-class across every creative surface. Every `AdBrief` carries a `CreativeAngle` field; brand guides can declare `preferred_angles` / `forbidden_angles` (with a schema-enforced contradiction check — a brand can't both prefer and forbid the same angle); and `CheckBrief` runs a uniform pre-generation audit that blocks forbidden-for-brand angles (IssueError) and warns on angle/format mismatches (IssueWarn). The scoring rubric gains two angle-aware checks (`angle_declared` as a core gate, `angle_format_compatible` as polish), and `CreativeRubricSpec.WithAngleCriterion` appends a vision-judge `angle_execution` criterion (weight 2) containing the angle's canonical rubric hints so the judge has concrete signal. Wisdom telemetry tracks angles as a parallel dimension to hooks: `WisdomAdEntry.Angle` rides into the anonymized ingest, and the aggregator buckets angle performance (ctr / cpc / roas / win / cpa / n) per format across all users — omitted entirely when no ads declare an angle so legacy installs aren't diluted. Ad names encode the angle suffix (`/ angle:{key}`) for round-trip inference, with case-insensitive alias resolution (`How_It_Works` → `mechanism`). REGRESSION GUARD: every creative-gen path that constructs an `AdBrief` must call `CheckBrief` before generation — forbidden-angle veto is a compliance fail, not a warning. Full test coverage: `angle_test.go` (17 tests), `format_brief_check_test.go` (per-format minimal briefs + angle checks), `creative_rubric_test.go` (angle criterion append + non-mutation), `brand_guide_test.go` (+6 angle schema tests), `wisdom_test.go` (angle suffix + inference round-trip), `wisdom-api/worker.test.mjs` (+3 angle aggregation tests). `go test ./...` and `node --test worker.test.mjs` both green.

v1.15.0: PWA + Inline Artifacts + Refine Harness. Phone PWA shipped a self-install flow — `pwa.merlingotme.com` is now its own subdomain (Cloudflare Worker), the QR modal pre-warms so it opens with no broken-image flash, the install-banner prompts iOS/Android users with platform-specific copy, and the voice pill went through three rounds of polish (iOS-glass body → brand-gradient dot → single-row dot+title with subtle sublabel and collapsed status text). The mobile relay fallback now surfaces inline in the QR modal so a user on flaky LAN sees "connecting via relay…" instead of a silent loop. Inline artifact gallery: every image / video / voice generation pipeline now emits a sentinel-wrapped JSON bundle that the renderer turns into an enterprise-grade gallery card right in the chat — no more "can you render those here?" round-trips. The Go binary, the parser (`app/artifact-parser.js`), and the renderer's `merlinUrl` are all idempotent on already-encoded paths after a Gitar finding caught a `%20 → %2520` regression that would have broken any filename containing a space round-tripping through the gallery (full test coverage: round-trip, mixed-encoding, literal-`%`-in-filename). Archive cards now show the human-readable model name under each title ("Banana Pro Edit" instead of `banana-pro-edit`) plus a quick-delete button on image/video cards. Brand-switch IPC was hardened against an in-flight `startSession` race that could leak the previous brand's config into the new session's first message. MCP gained reliability primitives (idempotency tokens, concurrency caps, envelope versioning) plus Meta-specific intent tools (`metaFindCampaign`, `metaFindAdSet`) and a rate-limit stress gate in CI. Voice recovers from ONNX backend init failures and truncated-webm bursts instead of silently dropping the recording. Skills now pin `banana-pro-edit` / `seedance-2` so Claude reports what actually ran instead of inventing model names.

Refine harness (Go side) shipped a major reliability + quality push: skip-judge mode for fast iteration, multi-retry fal preflight with jittered backoff (cures the thundering-herd ParallelFetch failures from the corpus sweep), scored-only pass-rate so SkipJudge runs surface real errors instead of silently inflating success metrics, fake-press-logo guard, and shell-locked image prompts grounded in edit-mode artifacts. Format shells hardened against residual gaps in `ugc_static`, `before_after`, `carousel_frame`, and `problem_solution` — the structural gaps that survived the v1.14.0 sweep got fixed in `creative_format.go:PromptAddendum` (per the format-shell-addendum rule, not in briefs or rubrics). Phase 2 corpus expansion (12 → 20+ DTC formats per brand) for the regression-sweep harness. Server-driven model registry with latest-per-capability resolution — when a new banana / seedance / claude family ships, registry routes through it without a binary rebuild. Relay worker allowlists `transcribe-audio` / `transcription` envelopes so the desktop voice tools can offload Whisper work to the relay when the local CPU is saturated. Six new SEO blog posts in `landing/blog-posts.js` (`gomarble-alternative`, `smartly-alternative`, `revealbot-alternative`, `madgicx-alternative`, `hyperfx-alternative`, `martha-ai-alternative`) for competitor-intercept search traffic.

v1.14.0: Decisions + Spellbook + App Hardening. Every kill / scale decision Merlin makes now lands as a signed DecisionFact in `activity.jsonl` — an HMAC-chained audit record that says exactly what changed, why, and which run triggered it. The Spellbook panel reads those facts and paints a one-line outcome under every spell row (e.g. "killed meta-ad 12345 · CTR 0.4% · spent $18.40" instead of a bare timestamp), so you can see at a glance what your autonomous runs actually DID without opening the activity log. Decisions also get an MCP queue registration + aligned trigger vocabulary (`ad_underperforming`, `ad_scaling_candidate`, `budget_exceeded`, etc.) so skills route to the right decision path. Phone pairing is now re-pair-safe — v1.13.x could throw `multi_device_pairing_pending` when a user re-paired an already-linked phone; the `/pair/mint` flow rotates the device binding cleanly and the relay worker is deployed to merlin-relay with the new endpoint. Scheduled-task setup (merlin-daily / merlin-optimize / merlin-digest / merlin-memory) now requires explicit user consent via a prompt card before any cron fires — no more silent task creation on first-run. App-surface hardening: new SSRF guard on brand scraping (refuses private-network IPs, metadata endpoints, non-http schemes), TTS validation rejects malformed voice IDs before they hit the worker, redact / tool tests cover MCP input sanitization end-to-end, spellbook logic extracted to its own module (`app/spellbook.js` + `spell-outcomes.js`) with 400+ lines of coverage, service worker carries a version constant so cached PWA assets bust correctly on update. Backend test coverage jumped across the board: router/merge (3 → 30+ tests), SEO + landing-audit (0 → 60), comms + creative AI (0 → 54), email + SMS preflight + TCPA + auth records (0 → 58 tests), blog validation, Wisdom API idempotency, plus httptest runtime coverage for tiktok / googleads / reddit / etsy. Three blog draft titles tightened to sub-70-char SEO-safe lengths. Vendor-cards validator now refuses docs_urls pointing at rate-limited API hosts (would've been a silent monthly-cron regression) and CRLF-normalizes check-mode comparisons so Windows contributors don't trip false diffs.

v1.13.0: Vendor Capability Cards — every integration (HeyGen, fal, ElevenLabs, Meta, TikTok, Google Ads, Shopify, Klaviyo) now ships with a standardized routing card baked into the domain skill: Pick when / Skip when / Killer features / Constraints / Cost / Output / Docs / Last verified. The LLM previously had action-level context (here are the actions you can call) but thin capability-level context (why HeyGen Hyperframes is the right pick for a multi-scene SaaS demo, or why Spark Ads beat branded-post uploads on TikTok); cards close that gap without bloating cold-start context (they live inside SKILL bodies, load on-demand when Claude routes into a skill, leave description budget untouched). Source of truth is `tools/vendor-cards/vendor-capabilities.json` (dev-only, does NOT ship via extraResources); `gen-vendor-cards.js` deterministically injects fenced `<!-- VENDOR-CARDS:BEGIN/END -->` blocks into the owning domain SKILL.md. New CI validator (`test/validate-vendor-cards.js`) enforces schema (≥2 pick_when, ≥2 skip_when, ≥2 killer_features per card), cross-repo action coverage against every `case` in `autocmo-core/main.go`, hedge-word hygiene, and generator-sync (editing the JSON without regenerating fails CI). Passive monthly drift detection (`.github/workflows/vendor-docs-drift.yml` + `test/vendor-docs-drift.js`) runs on GitHub infrastructure — never on user machines — hashing each vendor's docs_url, opening a draft PR when any page changes so Ryan can review the vendor's actual docs before shipping updated cards. MVP ships 8 vendors spread across merlin-content (HeyGen / fal / ElevenLabs), merlin-ads (Meta / TikTok / Google Ads), and merlin-ecom (Shopify / Klaviyo); new cards land through the same JSON → regenerate → validator → `/update` pipeline.

v1.12.1: Fact-binding production fix — the v1.12.0 rollout flipped the feature flag on but the renderer's fact-binding helpers were calling `require('./facts/facts-cache')`, `./facts/verify-facts`, and `./facts/chart-renderer` at runtime. In production the Electron renderer runs under `contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false, sandbox: false` — there is NO `require`, NO `Buffer`, NO `fs` in that world. Every one of those calls threw `ReferenceError: require is not defined`, caught silently by try/catch, and fact-binding became a no-op for every paying user on v1.12.0 despite the flag being on. The fix moves all three facts modules into `app/preload.js` (Node context, loaded once at window creation) and exposes an opaque-handle bridge through `window.merlinFactBinding` — `createCache` / `closeCache` / `watchFactsFile` / `stopWatcher` / `runAllPasses` / `mountCharts` / `createTailQuarantine` / `tailPush` / `tailFinalize`. The renderer holds integer handles; preload resolves them to the real FactCache / TailQuarantine instances and runs the HMAC / pass-1/2/3 / chart-mount logic in Node. Uint8Array→Buffer conversion moves from renderer (where Buffer is undefined) to preload. `chart-renderer.js` gains a string-based `mountChartsInHtml(html)` analogue because DOM elements can't cleanly cross contextBridge. REGRESSION GUARD (2026-04-18) comments in renderer.js and preload.js explain why `require` and `Buffer` are forbidden on the renderer side; new `renderer-bridge-runtime.test.js` (14 tests) source-scans for banned patterns AND drives the full bridge surface end-to-end. Full test suite 90/90.

v1.12.0: Fact-binding rollout — every number Merlin quotes is now cryptographically tied to the tool call that produced it. Each platform / analytics / revenue call emits HMAC-SHA256-signed fact envelopes (content-addressed over RFC8785-canonical JSON, keyed off a per-session HKDF-derived vault key), and the renderer runs a 3-pass interceptor on every assistant message: pass 1 substitutes `{{fact:<id>}}` tokens for verified `<span data-fact>` HTML, pass 2 binds `<div data-chart-config>` placeholders to signed chart payloads rendered as inline SVG (zero deps — no Chart.js weight in the installer), pass 3 scans any bare numeric literal not already bound to a fact and quarantines the suffix until the streaming tail either finalizes or trips the 2s slow-drip deadline. Send-boundary verifier re-checks HTML / plain / Slack blocks / Discord embeds against the signed fact log before anything leaves the app — fabricated ROAS, revenue, CAC, CTR, growth %, multipliers, and implied derivations all get flagged. `featureFlags.factBinding` flips on by default in this release (dual-switch override still available via window.__merlinFactBindingForceOn or MERLIN_FACT_BINDING=1 for kill-switch rollback). New protected-path patterns in block-api-bypass.js cover `.merlin-facts-key` and `.merlin-facts.<sessionID>.jsonl` (plus .bak/.tmp siblings per Rule 7) so the HMAC key + signed envelope log can't be read, copied, or rewritten mid-session. Chart renderer's empty-data fallback now escapes the title (XSS regression guard test added). Streaming boundary tests cover split tokens, split currency literals, tail-flush, and 2s slow-drip adversarial inputs; perf gate on the verifier hits P50 0.14ms / P95 0.35ms / P99 0.48ms on a 40-fact / 5000-word / 3-table / 1-chart fixture (budget was P50≤20 / P95≤100 / P99≤250). Goal: zero hallucinated metrics reach a paying user, full stop.

v1.11.1: Foreplay tile in the Magic panel. The competitor-ad-spying integration shipped in v1.8.0 was accessible only by hand-editing merlin-config.json — now it's a BYOK tile in the Universal section (one key covers every brand). Click to paste your Foreplay API key (link opens the key-management page in your browser), right-click a connected tile to disconnect. Matches the fal/elevenlabs/heygen/arcads pattern: `foreplayApiKey` added to the renderer's API_KEY_PLATFORMS map + main.js CONFIG_FIELD_ALLOWLIST + getConnections detection + disconnect-platform keyMap + GLOBAL_KEYS_SET so disconnects scope correctly to the global config. No binary changes required — foreplay.go already reads `cfg.ForeplayAPIKey`, so existing users who previously set the key manually see the tile turn green immediately on upgrade.

v1.11.0: Variant Tournament skill — adversarial creative iteration that beats LLM self-revision drift. New `merlin-tournament` SKILL routes a four-role loop (critic teardown → blind author who never sees the incumbent → synthesizer producing A / AB-merge / B → 3 blind judges scoring via Borda count with shuffled labels) plus a hard k=2 stop rule (incumbent wins twice → converged, stop iterating). The point: when a user wants to *beat* an existing winning ad, the old `write me 10 variations` flow produced cosmetic rewrites of the same hook because the LLM anchors to whatever it sees. Withholding the incumbent from Author B forces a genuinely different angle; anonymized labels keep judges from anchoring to the original; the k=2 stop rule prevents endless `improvement` rounds that flatten brand voice. Knowledge layer assembles from `dashboard` (current numbers), `dashboard wisdom` (cross-user benchmarks), brand `memory.md` + `brand.md` (customer language), and `competitor_spy` longest-running ads (proven-spend swipe file) — all four cached per tournament so every round sees the same baseline. Cross-references wired in `merlin-ads` Promotion Gate (route challenger generation here, not back to `merlin-content`) and `commands/merlin.md` fuzzy-intent table. Anti-drift guarantees are non-negotiable rules in the skill body — bypassing any of them defeats the pattern.

v1.10.0: Google Merchant Center integration — the missing piece for Google Shopping ads. One `google-login` now grants three scopes in a single consent (Ads + Search Console + Merchant Center via `auth/content`); the Merchant Center ID is auto-discovered and persisted as `googleMerchantId`. Four new MCP actions under `mcp__merlin__google_ads`: `merchant-status` (approved / disapproved / pending counts + top 5 disapproval reasons — the #1 reason Shopping ads silently stop serving, and the thing Google Ads insights refuses to surface), `merchant-setup` (MCA multi-client account picker), `merchant-sync-shopify` (maps active Shopify products → Merchant product inputs via the new unified `productInputs:insert` endpoint; skips drafts / items missing title, price, or primary image; maps `compareAtPrice` → `salePrice`; derives availability from `inventory_quantity`; idempotent re-runs via `offerId = shopify-{productID}`), and `merchant-insights` (per-product clicks/impressions/CTR from the Merchant reports API via MQL). New `google_merchant` rate-limit bucket (60/min, 1.5K/hr, 20K/day) keeps a catalog sync from starving Google Ads calls on the same account. 401 auto-refresh shares the `googleRefreshMu` mutex with Ads so a single token rotation covers every Google surface. Zero new OAuth providers, zero new consent screens — the expanded scope rides the existing Google login. Full test coverage: 11 merchant-specific tests including a regression guard that fails the build if the `auth/content` scope gets dropped from the OAuth factory.

v1.9.0: Auto-generated brand guides at onboarding — a hidden Electron scrape captures palette/typography/logo/copy samples, Claude synthesizes a schema-enforced brand-guide.json via the new merlin-brand-guide skill (anti-slop forbidden-word guard + WCAG contrast math + concrete-only palette/typography/voice/imagery rules), and the user sees a live review card (palette swatches, font specimens rendered in real Google Fonts, voice DO/DON'T cards, email preview using their exact CTA shape + colors). Two new MCP tools: brand_scrape (BrowserWindow-based scraper returning weighted palette, typography stack, logo candidates, JSON-LD schema, copy samples, screenshots — default-elides the large payloads for context hygiene) and brand_guide (validate / write / read actions — write hits the Go validator for WCAG + forbidden-word + schema enforcement before atomic persist to assets/brands/<brand>/brand-guide.json). ipcMain read-brand-guide handler + preload readBrandGuide bridge so the review HTML can load the persisted JSON without direct filesystem access. Zero new API keys, zero new dependencies — Electron's bundled Chromium IS the scraper.

v1.8.0: Competitor ad intelligence via Foreplay global discovery. New `competitor_spy` MCP tool pulls ads for any brand worldwide WITHOUT pre-subscribing to them in the Foreplay UI — the agent routes exclusively through /api/brand/getBrandsByDomain, getAdsByBrandId, getAdsByPageId, ad/duplicates, and ad/{id}. Fills the US-coverage gap that Meta Ads Library's EU-only preview restriction leaves open. Supports ad-media download (video/image → results/competitor-ads/<ad_id>.<ext>), ad-duplicate reverse lookup, cursor-based pagination, and credit-usage telemetry. BYOK — user supplies their own `foreplayApiKey` (0.01 credits per ad returned). Rate-limited via PreflightCheck (30/min, 500/hr, 5000/day). Bearer key banned from any direct outbound call via block-api-bypass.js — only the binary can touch public.api.foreplay.co. Static-source guards pin the no-Spyder contract (foreplay_test.go:TestForeplayOnlyGlobalDiscoveryEndpoints) — a future refactor that routes through /api/spyder/* fails the build.

v1.7.0: The big UX + resilience release. OAuth tokens now auto-refresh across every platform on a 4-hour Electron watchdog heartbeat plus a per-platform refresh loop in the Go core — no more reconnect every time a token expires. Every brand remembers its own conversation thread and paints prior history on reopen instead of a blank welcome. Morning briefing card summarizes ad performance, content, revenue, and a recommended next move at session start. Starter chips on both new and returning flows, Undo toast for ad kills, image-only Share, Ctrl/Cmd+D mic toggle, Ctrl/Cmd+S narrate-last, Lavender Pearl light mode with a titlebar sun/moon toggle, branded purple/green ad placeholders replacing the old gray broken-image stub, Stripe read-only revenue reporting, Reddit organic prospecting with a 7-layer compliance preflight, streaming TTS (first spoken word in ~400-700ms vs 2-3s), and a SaaS-grade rubric upgrade across every skill. Cold-start warmup is parallelized and pre-warms the SDK subprocess in the background so first-message latency drops from ~1-3s cold to immediate. Dashboard JSON parses are cached on the async hot path. Shopify now requests write_content and surfaces scope gaps as a reconnect prompt instead of a raw 403. Google Ads tiles stay green reliably across disconnect/reconnect cycles. Auto-claim referral on first launch. Under the hood: skill-routing migration from monolithic prose routers to 8 SKILLs loaded on demand (~53 KB off the cold-start context), monthly audit fail-loud on missing secrets, CI asar size ceiling, rate-limit preflight hardening via NextSlotNano, hook blocklist atomic-write siblings, framework-file protection so skill descriptions can't be hot-rewritten mid-session. Wisdom-api migrations now run once per isolate instead of per request.
whatsNew: Fixed: clicking Save on the Postscript or AppLovin (MAX / AppDiscovery) tile in the Magic panel could silently do nothing — modal stayed open, no toast, no error. Both tiles now save correctly to the vault.
Fixed: any future tile-save rejection now displays the actual error inline in the modal instead of silently swallowing it. No more 'I clicked Save and nothing happened.'
Hardened: a new CI test cross-checks every BYOK tile in renderer.js against the config allowlist + vault list, so the next added tile can't ship with the same omission.
